content.full_text:
CalifOrnia
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
Apri■ ■9, ■990
Honorable Doug Vagin
Supervisor
county of Fresno
P.O. Box 4042
Fresno, CA 937 44
Re: Your Request for Infomal Assistance
our File No' I-90-207
Dear Supervisor Vagim:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice concern-
ing your responsibi l ities under the conf l ict-of-interest 
provi-
.i6"! "i the PoliticaL Reform Act (the "Actu1.1 
Your letter
seeks fol1ow-up advice regarding our advice letter to you of
February 15, 1990. (Vaqim Advice Letter, No. I-89-688, copy
enclosed.) Since your question is general in nature we are treat-
ing your request a3 one for informal assistance.2
OUESTION
May you participate in future decisions of the Fresno county
Board oi Supeivisors concerning the Fresno Metropolitan Flood
control District?
CONCLUS ION
Absent facts to indicate an independent contractual relation-
ship between you and the Fresno Metropolitan Flood control
oislrict, 
the district 
woul,d not be treated as a source of income
to you. Thus, you may participate in decisions concerning the
Fresno Metropolitan Flood control District.
1 cor".rr."nt code Sections 81ooo-91015. AIl statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated. 
Cornmission
regulations appear at 2 California code of Regulations Section
18ooo, et seo. A11 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations,
2 _-
- Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
imruunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Govern-
ment Code Section 83114; 2 cal. Code of Regs. section
1832s (c) (3) . )
428 J street, Suite 8oo . P.o. Box 8o7 o sacramento cA 958o,l.ogo7 o (916) 3 22-5660


File No. l-9O-2O7
Page 2
Please note, however, that other provisions of 1aw, such as
Section 1090, may restrict your ability to participate in these
decisions. Section 1090 is not within the Political 
Reform Act,
and is, therefore, outside the conmission's jurisdiction. 
You
should contact the Attorney ceneral's office for guidance concern-
ing that section.
rACTS
You are the Supervisor for District 3 of Fresno county. rn
your private capacity you provide computer services to the Fresno
Irletropolitan Flood Control District (the itdistrictrr) in conjunc-
tion with your brother's business, Alphameric Data Processing
(hereaf ter I'Alphamericr' ) .
The parties to the contract for provision of computer
services to the district are the district 
and Alphameric.
However, you were designated in the initial 
bid as also providing
services to the district 
on behalf of Alphaneric. You described
the payment procedure in connection rrith the contract as follolrs:
You subnit a listing of charges to ALphaneric, Al-phameric then
submits a billing to the district 
which includes your charges, the
district 
then writes a check to Alphaneric, and Alphaneric
compensates your for you services fron the proceeds of the check.
You have asked uhether you may participate j.n the fo11o$ring
decisions of the Fresno county Board of Supervisors:
a. The distribution of property tax augrentation funds
to the district,'
b. Proposal to expand the jurisdiction of the district
to other unincorporated regions of the county;
c. Transfer of part of the county's property tax base
to the district 
lrhere the district's 
jurisdiction is
expanded.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 of the Act provides:
No public official 
at any leve1 of state or
locaI governnent shaIl nake, participate in making
or in any hray attenpt to use his official 
position
to influence a lrovernmental decision in which he
knows or has reason to know he has a financial
interest.
As a nenber of the Fresno 
_ County Board of Supervisors, you
are a rrpublic officialr 
as defined in the Act. 
(Section aioAA.l
Thus, you nay not use your official 
position to influence a
governnental decision in. which you know or have reason to know you
have a financial interest.


File No. I-90-2O7
Page 3
Section 87103 specifies that a public official 
has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision witl have a rnaterial financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public Aenerally, on the
official 
or a meuber of his or her i'n'nedi,ate fanily or on:
Any source of incone, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institu-
tion in the reg'ular course of business on terms
available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars
($250) or Eore in value provided to, received by or
promised to the public official within 12 nonths
prior to the tine when the decision is made.
Section 87103 (c) .
According to Section 87103, any person or business that has
rnade any payment to you is a source of income to you. In addi-
tion, under some circumstances rre have treated nultiple persons as
sources of incorne under a single contract. 
(Dorsev Advice Letter,
No. A-87-176, copy enclosed.) If, in the aggregate, the payments
are for $250 or more in the past 12 months, the source or sources
are potentially disgualifying financial interests for the purposes
of Section 87103.
In the previous letter to you rre concluded that Alphaneric
was the sole source of income to you under the contract between
the district 
and Alphameric. According to the information you
provided, the dj,strict had contracted with Alphaneric Data
Processing to provide conputer services to the district. 
You had
no ownership interest in Alphaneric, but only act as a consultant
on this contract. since you appeared to be acting as nerely a
subcontractor to Alphaneric in providinq services to the district
we concluded that the contractor, Alphaneric, was the sole source
of income to you, despite the fact that you are paid from funds
collected fron the district. 
(See e.q., Hart Advice Letter, No.
\-83-264 , copy enclosed. )
This conclusion is further supported by the fact that the
contract does not appear to create any contractual relationship
bet$reen the district 
and yourself, and only mentions you in the
context of a description of the team to provide services on behalf
of Alphaneric. Moreover, your payrnent flows from Alphaneric which
also suggests that the business relationship is actually bete/een
you and Alphameric.
Thus, absent facts to indicate an independent contractual
relationship between you and the district, 
we reaffirn our prior
conclusion that the district 
is not a source of income to y^ou.
consequently, the political 
Reforn Act does not prohibit you frorn
participating in decisions 
_ concerning the distri-ct. 
plea3e note,
hohrever, that other provisj,ons of larr, such as Section 1090, may
ヽ
　
　
‘


一
　
●
File No. I-9O-2O7
Page 4
restrict your ability to participate in these decisions. Section
1090 is not within the Politica1 Reform Act, and is therefore,
outside the conuission's jurisdiction. 
You should contact the
Attorney ceneral's Office for guidance concerning that section.
If you have any further questions regarding this uatter,
please.feeI free to contact ne at (915) 322-5901.
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General counsel
